Title: Trans-acting small interfering RNA4: key to nutraceutical synthesis in grape development?

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: small RNA-mediated post-transcriptional silencing via miR828-triggered tasiRNA biogenesis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely promotes nutraceutical polyphenolic/flavonoid biosynthesis and fruit developmental regulation by silencing specific MYB repressible nodes; proposed key effector in grape berry development
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Proposed master regulator/key effector controlling bioflavonoid synthesis and fruit development in grape through tasiRNA-mediated control of MYB transcription factors
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Negatively regulates VvMYBA6 and VvMYBA7 through TAS4-derived tasiRNAs, especially TAS4b-3'D4(-)
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: biosynthesis of polyphenolic nutraceuticals/flavonoids; fruit development
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Fruit color, flavor/astringency, antioxidant and anti-inflammatory nutraceutical properties, roles in stress adaptation and development
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: All three grape TAS4 loci are expressed; TAS4a and TAS4b have EST support, TAS4c is much lower expressed
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: TAS4 functions in leaves, flowers, and fruits; TAS4b species showed reproducible abundance across multiple grape libraries
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Phosphate starvation; sugars; ABA; water deficit/water stress; pathogen or virus infection discussed as relevant context
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Autoregulatory/homeostatic feedback loop with targeted MYBs is discussed
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: TAS non-coding RNA locus producing phased tasiRNAs
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Computational analysis of sRNA-seq and degradome datasets; phasiRNA mapping in phase with predicted TAS4 cleavage site; comparative expression/phylogenetic analysis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TAS4 is proposed in this opinion article as a key effector for grape nutraceutical polyphenol synthesis and fruit development. 2. Grapevine TAS4-derived tasiRNAs, particularly TAS4b-3'D4(-), are supported by sRNA-seq/degradome evidence to target and silence VvMYBA6 and VvMYBA7. 3. TAS4 is linked to sugar, ABA, and phosphate-responsive regulatory networks relevant to berry development and stress/pathogen responses.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential RNAi/molecular breeding target for improving fruit nutraceutical quality, flavor, berry development, stress adaptation, and possibly disease resistance
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review/opinion article; much of the grape functional assignment is inferential/correlative rather than direct transgenic validation. Three grape loci are discussed: TAS4a, TAS4b, TAS4c; TAS4b is inferred to be the major trigger of VvMYBA6/7 phasiRNAs.

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional regulation of flavonoid/polyphenol pathway genes; itself post-transcriptionally silenced by TAS4-derived tasiRNA
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Proposed effector controlling grape fruit pigment synthesis and broader bioflavonoid metabolism during berry development
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Suggested regulator in hierarchy of MYBs controlling anthocyanin-related fruit pigmentation and nutraceutical metabolism, potentially contributing to anthocyanin variation unexplained by VvMYBA1-3
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Inferred to regulate pathway modules for anthocyanin, proanthocyanidin, flavonol and/or lignin-related metabolism based on phylogenetic placement
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: polyphenolic/flavonoid biosynthesis; fruit pigment synthesis; berry development
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pigmentation, antioxidant nutraceutical activity, berry quality traits
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Supported as targeted in leaves, flowers, and fruits via phasiRNA datasets
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Phylogenetic analysis; computational small RNA/phasiRNA mapping; association/correlative evidence from anthocyanin variation and berry development context
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VvMYBA6 is identified as a novel grape MYB target of TAS4-derived tasiRNAs. 2. It likely functions as an effector in bioflavonoid synthesis based on phylogenetic position among flavonoid-regulating MYBs. 3. It may help explain anthocyanin variation and véraison-associated berry traits not accounted for by VvMYBA1-3 alone.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate marker/gene for association mapping and molecular breeding of anthocyanin content, berry quality, and possibly disease-related developmental responses
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Function is proposed mainly from phylogenetic and correlative evidence in this opinion piece, not direct mechanistic validation of downstream targets.

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional regulation of flavonoid/polyphenol pathway genes; itself post-transcriptionally silenced by TAS4-derived tasiRNA
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Proposed effector controlling grape fruit pigment synthesis and broader bioflavonoid metabolism during berry development
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Suggested regulator in hierarchy of MYBs controlling anthocyanin-related fruit pigmentation and nutraceutical metabolism, potentially contributing to anthocyanin variation unexplained by VvMYBA1-3
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Inferred to regulate pathway modules for anthocyanin, proanthocyanidin, flavonol and/or lignin-related metabolism based on phylogenetic placement
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: polyphenolic/flavonoid biosynthesis; fruit pigment synthesis; berry development
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pigmentation, antioxidant nutraceutical activity, berry quality traits
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Fruits/berries; also relevant to leaves and flowers
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Supported as targeted in leaves, flowers, and fruits via phasiRNA and degradome datasets
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Computational small RNA/phasiRNA mapping; degradome support; phylogenetic analysis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VvMYBA7 is identified as a novel grape MYB target of TAS4-derived tasiRNAs. 2. Degradome and phasiRNA evidence support TAS4-dependent cleavage/transitivity rather than direct miR828 action. 3. It is proposed to function in berry bioflavonoid metabolism and development.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate gene for breeding or engineering berry pigmentation, nutraceutical quality, and developmental traits
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Function is inferred, not directly demonstrated by transgenic perturbation in this paper.

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: initiates TAS4 tasiRNA biogenesis and directly cleaves some MYB transcripts in some species
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts upstream of TAS4-centered regulation of flavonoid/polyphenol metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Provides the trigger required for TAS4 tasiRNA production, thereby enabling silencing of MYB regulators of nutraceutical pathways
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Triggers cleavage of TAS4 transcripts and in some contexts directly targets MYB transcripts; in grape direct action on VvMYBA6/7 is argued to be unlikely
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pigmentation, nutraceutical antioxidant functions, stress/development responses
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Virus infection; phosphate status; sugars; ABA discussed in the network context
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Literature synthesis plus computational target analysis in grape datasets
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. miR828 is the trigger for TAS4 tasiRNA biogenesis. 2. In grape, the paper argues miR828 likely does not directly target VvMYBA6 and VvMYBA7 because of seed mismatches. 3. miR828/TAS4 together form a conserved regulatory module impacting flavonoid-related MYBs.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential upstream small RNA module for manipulating fruit nutraceutical composition and developmental traits via RNAi strategies
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core regulator because the review centers on the miR828-TAS4-MYB module; however the paper emphasizes TAS4 as the key effector.
